finding: integer division makes the FILE_TOO_LARGE message read 'File size (1 MiB) exceeds the maximum of 1 MiB' for a file just over a small cap — self-contradictory
same integer-division message truncation; also backend uses MiB while frontend label uses MB for the identical binary cap value
MAX_FILE_UPLOAD_LABEL='128MB' presents the binary 128 MiB cap as MB while the backend error said MiB — cross-surface unit inconsistency
MAX_FILE_UPLOAD_LABEL is a hardcoded literal decoupled from MAX_FILE_UPLOAD_BYTES; the two can silently drift
label must be manually kept in sync with the byte constant; derive it from bytes to prevent drift

no test sends a body above the derived route body limit (cap+16 MiB) or asserts 413, so routes.rs/project_extension/routes.rs reading file_upload_body_limit_bytes() is unverified — reverting it to a constant would still pass
max_file_upload_mb has no server-side upper bound; with full-RAM multipart buffering, a large admin-set cap enables memory-exhaustion DoS under concurrency
raising the accepted cap 50->128 MiB amplifies per-upload RAM under concurrency since the whole body is buffered; derived route body limit actually dropped 200->144 MiB
config doc comment cites genomics files as '80-200 MB+' (decimal) while the cap is defined in MiB
reviewed the boot-order (set before build_api_router in both main.rs and lib.rs), mb->bytes saturating conversion, and derived body-limit > cap; no correctness defect found
reviewed the Lazy<Mutex<usize>> global: lock never held across await, set-once-at-boot happens-before per-request reads, only one unit test mutates it; no race
source description const matches the committed openapi.json (ui+desktop); operation description does not flow into types.ts so types_ts_parity is safe; no schema change
removed consts have no remaining referencers; the two exhaustive TestServerOptions initializers were updated; core::mod re-export has no collision; no regression
the too-large toast keeps the existing message.error pattern; no accessible-name or focus regression introduced
the race-tolerant 413 test's Err branch accepts any non-timeout transport error, so a server crash/reset mid-upload could be misclassified as a body-limit rejection
